suggestions.title: Duplicated getTeamVibe logic in App.jsx and Office.jsx
suggestions.description: getTeamVibe is defined twice — once in App.jsx (returns {label, key} object) and once in Office.jsx (returns a plain string key). The threshold logic is identical but the return shapes differ, creating a silent divergence risk. Extract to a shared src/utils/vibe.js module and import in both files. This is a correctness bug waiting to happen when someone updates one copy and forgets the other.

suggestions.title: office-floor has role=img but contains an interactive element
suggestions.description: The .office-floor div carries role='img' (line 392 of Office.jsx), but Bloberto (.char--manager) has pointer-events: auto and triggers mouse events — it is interactive. Placing interactive elements inside role='img' violates ARIA authoring practices and will confuse screen readers. Change role='img' to role='region' with the same aria-label, and add aria-live='polite' so screen readers announce vibe changes.

suggestions.title: getTeamVibe called twice on every render in App.jsx header
suggestions.description: Lines 510–511 of App.jsx call getTeamVibe(activeWorkers) twice in the same render pass (once for data-vibe attribute, once for the label text). This is redundant computation. Hoist the call to a const vibe = useMemo(() => getTeamVibe(activeWorkers), [activeWorkers]) and reference vibe.key and vibe.label where needed.

suggestions.title: Polling silently pauses after idle timeout with no user feedback
suggestions.description: After IDLE_TIMEOUT (10 min) of inactivity, pollTick silently stops syncing. The UI shows stale data and the Live indicator may still appear green because isLive is not reset. Users have no idea the feed is paused. Add a visual 'Paused — move mouse to resume' chip in the header or over the live-dot, and reset isLive=false when the idle timeout kicks in.

suggestions.title: Office tab shows no loading state and no error state
suggestions.description: The Dashboard tab has LoadingSkeleton while isLoading=true, but the Office tab just renders an empty floor with only Bloberto. Similarly, if fetchError fires the error banner only appears on the Dashboard; switching to Office shows nothing wrong. Pass isLoading and hasError flags to Office and render a simple overlay or placeholder text so the Office tab communicates its state.

suggestions.title: Character data-tooltip is CSS-only and invisible to screen readers
suggestions.description: Tooltip text on worker characters is rendered via .char[data-tooltip]::after which is purely visual. Screen readers will not announce it. Working characters show their current task; ghost characters show their role — this information is entirely inaccessible. Add a title attribute matching the data-tooltip value, or implement a proper tooltip with role='tooltip' and aria-describedby. Also, Bloberto's speech bubble fades in with no ARIA announcement.

suggestions.title: Simultaneous CSS animations with no prefers-reduced-motion guard
suggestions.description: Up to 8 concurrent animations can run at once: manager-wobble, bob, wander-1/2/3/4, vibe-crush/fire, error-halo, ghost-float, standby-pulse, pulse-live, shimmer, card-rise. On low-powered devices this is a battery and performance drain. Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to disable or freeze all cosmetic animations (wanders, wobble, bob, pulses) while keeping functionally meaningful ones like the error-halo.

suggestions.title: Inline styles in JSX should be CSS classes
suggestions.description: WorkerCard uses style={{ flex: 1, paddingLeft: '0.75rem' }} inside JSX (line 186), and multiple section headers use style={{ marginTop: '2.5rem' }} and similar (lines 573, 608). Inline styles bypass the CSS variable system and cannot respond to theme changes or media queries. Move these to named CSS classes in App.css.

suggestions.title: seenIdsRef never resets — returning workers never get the new-arrival animation
suggestions.description: seenIdsRef accumulates every worker ID ever seen in the current session (line 452). When a worker clocks out and returns, their ID is already in the set so firstTime is empty and the green pulse animation never fires again. For a live dashboard that cycles workers in and out, this means the visual confirmation of arrival only ever works once. Reset or expire seenIdsRef entries, or key on a session identifier from the data payload.

suggestions.title: Empty .progress-wrapper {} ruleset is dead CSS
suggestions.description: Line 439 of App.css has '.progress-wrapper {}' with no declarations inside. This is dead code left over from a refactor. Remove it to keep the stylesheet clean. Minor, but it signals incomplete cleanup.

suggestions.title: Activity log key is fragile — risk of React duplicate key warnings
suggestions.description: ActivityLog uses key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i} (line 102). The index fallback i masks potential duplicate key collisions when two events share the same timestamp and worker name (e.g. rapid re-hires). Request the API to include a stable unique id field on each activity entry, then use that as the React key.

suggestions.title: Idle wander paths overlap desk area — workers clip through furniture
suggestions.description: Wander keyframes 1–4 use top values as low as 73–74% which overlaps the second row of desks (positioned at top: 50%). An idle worker can visually slide through a desk. Clamp all wander top values to 79% or higher so idle characters stay in the unobstructed bottom corridor of the floor. Also affects the mobile view where the aspect-ratio: auto layout changes the effective desk positions.
